Name: DFGTEST

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: GASKET_ID
Fields.Documentation: Gasket ID.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: TCM_WRSTALL_PRE_EN
Fields.Documentation: Enable TCM Write Stall. Stall the response phase of the write that makes the stream buffer full.

Fields.Name: TCM_WRSTALL_POST_EN
Fields.Documentation: Enable TCM Write Stall. Stall the response phase of the write that attempts a write to a full stream buffer.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_INT_TYPE
Fields.Documentation:
0: Level triggered interrupt
1: Edge triggered interrupt.

Fields.Name: OS_INT_TYPE
Fields.Documentation:
0: Level triggered interrupt
1: Edge triggered interrupt.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: STRM_PRIO_DISABLE
Fields.Documentation:
Disable Stream priority setting on all streams.
Use existing simple priority scheme. 

This is a temporary DEBUG bit, which will be removed in next RTL release.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Registers.Name: ASS_DFG_IS_CB_ENDADR0
Registers.Description: Input Stream Circular Buffer End Address

Fields.Name: IS_CB_END_ADR
Fields.Description: Input Stream Circular Buffer End Address
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Circular Buffer End Address

Design updates this register on the rising edge of reset with predefined default values.

A connected PE writes this register to allocate a circular buffer while setting up a stream, whose size is
is_cb_end_adr - is_cb_st_adr.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_INT_ACK
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Interrupt Acknowledge

Decrements Interrupt Counter.
Increments CB read pointer by the interrupt size if interrupt is enabled and rdptr_ovd_dat_rcv_gsk is set.
This may effectively clear the interrupt if data available in the buffer falls below the thresholds set by buffer size and interrupt size.

Can be set by software. Rising edge on the corresponding interrupt ACK pin is equivalent to setting this bit. In both cases, this bit is auto cleared by hardware.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_RDPTR_OVD_DAT_RCV_GSK
Fields.Description: Input Stream Read Pointer Override Data Receive Gasket
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Read Pointer Override at the Data receive Gasket. (This gasket)

0: Read pointer is incremented by interrupt size on Return from Interrupt. Obeys PE's addressing mode.
    Indirect access Mode: Read pointer does not change.
    FIFO access Mode: Read pointer increments.

1: Read pointer increments when data is read from the circular buffer. Ignores PE's addressing mode.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_STRM_PRIO
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Priority.

Prioritize stream traffic on AXI bus. This also applies to the arbitration inside the gasket.

00: Default priority.

1,2: Higher priority.

3: Highest priority.

Streams at the same priority level are arbitrated using round robin scheme.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_STRM_ID
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream ID

Stream ID is virtual and not related to gasket ids. They are generated and maintained by software.
For proper gasket operation, following should be followed.

 -- All ID’s used by output streams should be unique in a gasket

 -- All ID’s used by input streams should be unique in a gasket.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_DST_INT_EN
Fields.Description: Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Enable
Fields.Documentation: Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Enable.

Fields.Name: IS_DST_INT_TRG
Fields.Description: Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Triggers
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Triggers

Bit0: Reserved.

Bit1: Interrupt on buffer size. First interrupt after stream is started happens when data of “buffer size” is received. Subsequent interrupts are issued whenever data of “interrupt size” is received. 

Bit2: Interrupt on interrupt size. Interrupt issued whenever data of “interrupt size” is received. 

Bit3: Reserved.

Only one bit should be set high for proper operation.

Fields.Name: IS_DST_INT_EN
Fields.Description: Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Enable
Fields.Documentation: Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Enable.

Fields.Name: IS_DST_INT_TRG
Fields.Description: Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Triggers
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Triggers

Bit0: Reserved.

Bit1: Interrupt on buffer size. First interrupt after stream is started happens when data of “buffer size” is received. Subsequent interrupts are issued whenever data of “interrupt size” is received. 

Bit2: Interrupt on interrupt size. Interrupt issued whenever data of “interrupt size” is received. 

Bit3: Reserved.

Only one bit should be set high for proper operation.

Fields.Name: IS_STRM_ID
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream ID

Stream ID is virtual and not related to gasket ids. They are generated and maintained by software.
For proper gasket operation, following should be followed.

 -- All ID’s used by output streams should be unique in a gasket

 -- All ID’s used by input streams should be unique in a gasket.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_DST_INT_SIZE
Fields.Description: Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Size
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Size in bytes.

Interrupt is asserted after number of bytes specified by this register are received, if enabled. 

Circular Buffer Read pointer is incremented by interrupt size on Return from Interrupt. This behavior can be overridden by setting rdptr_ovd_dat_rcv_gsk bit; when set the read pointer increments when data is read from the circular buffer.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_BUFFER_SIZE
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Buffer Size in bytes

(E.g., 39 tap filter: 39 words * chunk size)

Interrupt is asserted after number of bytes specified by this register are received, if enabled. This happens only for the first buffer of this size in the stream. This bitfield can also be used for non-filter related streams to assert interrupts.

Register streams: Size of the data to write or read.

Fields.Name: OS_BCAST_MASK
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Broadcast Destination Mask

Design can update it after receiving the header.

1-bit per gasket. 
Set to send the packet to a destination gasket. E.g., 64’h0f0, sends packet to gasket with ids 4,5,6 and 7.

Registers.Name: ASS_DFG_OS_CB_ENDADR0
Registers.Description: Output Stream Circular Buffer End Address

Fields.Name: OS_CB_END_ADR
Fields.Description: Output Stream Circular Buffer End Address
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Circular Buffer End Address

Design updates this register on the rising edge of reset with predefined default values.

A connected PE writes this register to allocate a circular buffer while setting up a stream, whose size is
os_cb_end_adr - os_cb_st_adr +1 bytes.

Buffer size should be a power of 2. 
To allocate a buffer size of K bytes, this bitfield should be programmed with os_cb_st_adr + K -1.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_CB_ST_ADR
Fields.Description: Output Stream Circular Buffer Start Address
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Circular Buffer Start Address

Design updates this register on the rising edge of reset with predefined default values.

A connected PE writes this register to allocate a circular buffer while setting up a stream, whose size is
os_cb_end_adr - os_cb_st_adr + 1 bytes.

Buffer size should be a power of 2.
To allocate a buffer size of K bytes, this bitfield should be programmed with a value which is an integer multiple of K.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_INT_ACK
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Interrupt Acknowledge

Increments CB write pointer by the interrupt size, if interrupt is enabled and wrptr_ovd_dat_xmt_gsk  is set.

Can be set by software. Rising edge on the corresponding interrupt ACK pin is equivalent to setting this bit. In both cases, this bit is auto cleared by hardware.

Output Stream Interrupt is cleared. If both buffer and interrupt size interrupt sources are set, buffer size interrupt source is cleared first in the hardware.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_WRPTR_OVD_DAT_XMT_GSK
Fields.Description: Output Stream Write Pointer Override Data Transmit Gasket
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Write Pointer Override at the Data transmit Gasket.

Used by source gasket for auto stream allocation.

0: Write pointer increments when data is written to the circular buffer. Ignores PE's addressing mode.

1: Write pointer is incremented by interrupt size on Return from Interrupt. Obeys PE's addressing mode.

    Indirect access Mode: Write pointer does not change.

    FIFO access Mode: Write pointer increments.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_C_BCAST_EN
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Cascaded Broadcast Enable

This broadcast is used to implement forking.

Fields.Name: OS_C_BCAST_FLAG
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Cascaded Broadcast Flag

0: Use the broadcast unit to implement forking.

1: Send every transaction individually to each destination.

Fields.Name: OS_STRM_PRIO
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Priority.

Prioritize stream traffic on AXI bus. This also applies to the arbitration inside the gasket.

00: Default priority.

1,2: Higher priority.

3: Highest priority.

Streams at the same priority level are arbitrated using round robin scheme.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_DST_GASKET_ID
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Destination Gasket ID.

This specifies ID of the gasket receiving the data transmitted by this gasket.

This field is ignored when cascaded broadcast enable is set.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_C_BCAST_FLAG
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Cascaded Broadcast Flag

0: Use the broadcast unit to implement forking.

1: Send every transaction individually to each destination.

Fields.Name: OS_STRM_PRIO
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Priority.

Prioritize stream traffic on AXI bus. This also applies to the arbitration inside the gasket.

00: Default priority.

1,2: Higher priority.

3: Highest priority.

Streams at the same priority level are arbitrated using round robin scheme.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_DST_GASKET_ID
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Destination Gasket ID.

This specifies ID of the gasket receiving the data transmitted by this gasket.

This field is ignored when cascaded broadcast enable is set.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_INT_ACK
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Interrupt Acknowledge

Increments CB write pointer by the interrupt size, if interrupt is enabled and wrptr_ovd_dat_xmt_gsk  is set.

Can be set by software. Rising edge on the corresponding interrupt ACK pin is equivalent to setting this bit. In both cases, this bit is auto cleared by hardware.

Output Stream Interrupt is cleared. If both buffer and interrupt size interrupt sources are set, buffer size interrupt source is cleared first in the hardware.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_WRPTR_OVD_DAT_XMT_GSK
Fields.Description: Output Stream Write Pointer Override Data Transmit Gasket
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Write Pointer Override at the Data transmit Gasket.

Used by source gasket for auto stream allocation.

0: Write pointer increments when data is written to the circular buffer. Ignores PE's addressing mode.

1: Write pointer is incremented by interrupt size on Return from Interrupt. Obeys PE's addressing mode.

    Indirect access Mode: Write pointer does not change.

    FIFO access Mode: Write pointer increments.

Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_STRM_ID
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream ID

Stream ID is virtual and not related to gasket ids. They are generated and maintained by software.
For proper gasket operation, following should be followed.

 -- All ID’s used by output streams should be unique in a gasket

 -- All ID’s used by input streams should be unique in a gasket.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_SRC_INT_EN
Fields.Description: Output Stream Data Source Interrupt Enable
Fields.Documentation: Output Stream Data Source Interrupt Enable.

Fields.Name: OS_SRC_INT_TRG
Fields.Description: Output Stream Data Source Interrupt Triggers
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Data Source Interrupt Triggers

Bit0: Reserved.

Bit1: Reserved.

Bit2: Interrupt on interrupt size. Interrupt issued whenever data of “interrupt size” is transmitted. When buffer size interrupt is enabled, first interrupt for this type happens when a data of buffer plus interrupt size is transmitted.

Bit3: Reserved.

Registers.Name: DSS_DFG_IS_CB_ENDADR0
Registers.Description: Input Stream Circular Buffer End Address

Fields.Name: IS_CB_END_ADR
Fields.Description: Input Stream Circular Buffer End Address
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Circular Buffer End Address

Design updates this register on the rising edge of reset with predefined default values.

A connected PE writes this register to allocate a circular buffer while setting up a stream, whose size is
is_cb_end_adr - is_cb_st_adr.

Fields.Name: IS_CB_ST_ADR
Fields.Description: Input Stream Circular Buffer Start Address
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Circular Buffer Start Address

Design updates this register on the rising edge of reset with predefined default values.

A connected PE writes this register to allocate a circular buffer while setting up a stream, whose size is
is_cb_end_adr - is_cb_st_adr.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_INT_ACK
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Interrupt Acknowledge

Decrements Interrupt Counter.
Increments CB read pointer by the interrupt size if interrupt is enabled and rdptr_ovd_dat_rcv_gsk is set.
This may effectively clear the interrupt if data available in the buffer falls below the thresholds set by buffer size and interrupt size.

Can be set by software. Rising edge on the corresponding interrupt ACK pin is equivalent to setting this bit. In both cases, this bit is auto cleared by hardware.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_RDPTR_OVD_DAT_RCV_GSK
Fields.Description: Input Stream Read Pointer Override Data Receive Gasket
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Read Pointer Override at the Data receive Gasket. (This gasket)

0: Read pointer is incremented by interrupt size on Return from Interrupt. Obeys PE's addressing mode.
    Indirect access Mode: Read pointer does not change.
    FIFO access Mode: Read pointer increments.

1: Read pointer increments when data is read from the circular buffer. Ignores PE's addressing mode.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_STRM_PRIO
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Priority.

Prioritize stream traffic on AXI bus. This also applies to the arbitration inside the gasket.

00: Default priority.

1,2: Higher priority.

3: Highest priority.

Streams at the same priority level are arbitrated using round robin scheme.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: RESERVED

Fields.Name: IS_STRM_PRIO
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Priority.

Prioritize stream traffic on AXI bus. This also applies to the arbitration inside the gasket.

00: Default priority.

1,2: Higher priority.

3: Highest priority.

Streams at the same priority level are arbitrated using round robin scheme.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_INT_ACK
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Interrupt Acknowledge

Decrements Interrupt Counter.
Increments CB read pointer by the interrupt size if interrupt is enabled and rdptr_ovd_dat_rcv_gsk is set.
This may effectively clear the interrupt if data available in the buffer falls below the thresholds set by buffer size and interrupt size.

Can be set by software. Rising edge on the corresponding interrupt ACK pin is equivalent to setting this bit. In both cases, this bit is auto cleared by hardware.

    FIFO access Mode: Read pointer increments.

1: Read pointer increments when data is read from the circular buffer. Ignores PE's addressing mode.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_STRM_PRIO
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Priority.

Prioritize stream traffic on AXI bus. This also applies to the arbitration inside the gasket.

00: Default priority.

1,2: Higher priority.

3: Highest priority.

Streams at the same priority level are arbitrated using round robin scheme.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_INT_ACK
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Interrupt Acknowledge

Decrements Interrupt Counter.
Increments CB read pointer by the interrupt size if interrupt is enabled and rdptr_ovd_dat_rcv_gsk is set.
This may effectively clear the interrupt if data available in the buffer falls below the thresholds set by buffer size and interrupt size.

Can be set by software. Rising edge on the corresponding interrupt ACK pin is equivalent to setting this bit. In both cases, this bit is auto cleared by hardware.

0: Read pointer is incremented by interrupt size on Return from Interrupt. Obeys PE's addressing mode.
    Indirect access Mode: Read pointer does not change.
    FIFO access Mode: Read pointer increments.

1: Read pointer increments when data is read from the circular buffer. Ignores PE's addressing mode.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_STRM_PRIO
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Priority.

Prioritize stream traffic on AXI bus. This also applies to the arbitration inside the gasket.

00: Default priority.

1,2: Higher priority.

3: Highest priority.

Streams at the same priority level are arbitrated using round robin scheme.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_INT_ACK
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Interrupt Acknowledge

Decrements Interrupt Counter.
Increments CB read pointer by the interrupt size if interrupt is enabled and rdptr_ovd_dat_rcv_gsk is set.
This may effectively clear the interrupt if data available in the buffer falls below the thresholds set by buffer size and interrupt size.

Can be set by software. Rising edge on the corresponding interrupt ACK pin is equivalent to setting this bit. In both cases, this bit is auto cleared by hardware.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_STRM_ID
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream ID

Stream ID is virtual and not related to gasket ids. They are generated and maintained by software.
For proper gasket operation, following should be followed.

 -- All ID’s used by output streams should be unique in a gasket

 -- All ID’s used by input streams should be unique in a gasket.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_DST_INT_EN
Fields.Description: Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Enable
Fields.Documentation: Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Enable.

Fields.Name: IS_DST_INT_TRG
Fields.Description: Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Triggers
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Triggers

Bit0: Reserved.

Bit1: Interrupt on buffer size. First interrupt after stream is started happens when data of “buffer size” is received. Subsequent interrupts are issued whenever data of “interrupt size” is received. 

Bit2: Interrupt on interrupt size. Interrupt issued whenever data of “interrupt size” is received. 

Bit3: Reserved.

Only one bit should be set high for proper operation.

Input Stream ID

Stream ID is virtual and not related to gasket ids. They are generated and maintained by software.
For proper gasket operation, following should be followed.

 -- All ID’s used by output streams should be unique in a gasket

 -- All ID’s used by input streams should be unique in a gasket.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_DST_INT_EN
Fields.Description: Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Enable
Fields.Documentation: Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Enable.

Fields.Name: IS_DST_INT_TRG
Fields.Description: Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Triggers
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Triggers

Bit0: Reserved.

Bit1: Interrupt on buffer size. First interrupt after stream is started happens when data of “buffer size” is received. Subsequent interrupts are issued whenever data of “interrupt size” is received. 

Bit2: Interrupt on interrupt size. Interrupt issued whenever data of “interrupt size” is received. 

Bit3: Reserved.

Only one bit should be set high for proper operation.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_DST_INT_SIZE
Fields.Description: Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Size
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Size in bytes.

Interrupt is asserted after number of bytes specified by this register are received, if enabled. 

Circular Buffer Read pointer is incremented by interrupt size on Return from Interrupt. This behavior can be overridden by setting rdptr_ovd_dat_rcv_gsk bit; when set the read pointer increments when data is read from the circular buffer.

Circular Buffer Read pointer is incremented by interrupt size on Return from Interrupt. This behavior can be overridden by setting rdptr_ovd_dat_rcv_gsk bit; when set the read pointer increments when data is read from the circular buffer.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_BUFFER_SIZE
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Buffer Size in bytes

(E.g., 39 tap filter: 39 words * chunk size)

Interrupt is asserted after number of bytes specified by this register are received, if enabled. This happens only for the first buffer of this size in the stream. This bitfield can also be used for non-filter related streams to assert interrupts.

Register streams: Size of the data to write or read.

Interrupt is asserted after number of bytes specified by this register are received, if enabled. This happens only for the first buffer of this size in the stream. This bitfield can also be used for non-filter related streams to assert interrupts.

Register streams: Size of the data to write or read.

Fields.Name: OS_BCAST_MASK
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Broadcast Destination Mask

Design can update it after receiving the header.

1-bit per gasket. 
Set to send the packet to a destination gasket. E.g., 64’h0f0, sends packet to gasket with ids 4,5,6 and 7.

Registers.Name: DSS_DFG_OS_CB_ENDADR0
Registers.Description: Output Stream Circular Buffer End Address

Fields.Name: OS_CB_END_ADR
Fields.Description: Output Stream Circular Buffer End Address
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Circular Buffer End Address

Design updates this register on the rising edge of reset with predefined default values.

A connected PE writes this register to allocate a circular buffer while setting up a stream, whose size is
os_cb_end_adr - os_cb_st_adr +1 bytes.

Buffer size should be a power of 2. 
To allocate a buffer size of K bytes, this bitfield should be programmed with os_cb_st_adr + K -1.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Registers.Name: DSS_DFG_OS_CB_ENDADR14
Registers.Description: Output Stream Circular Buffer End Address

Fields.Name: OS_CB_END_ADR
Fields.Description: Output Stream Circular Buffer End Address
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Circular Buffer End Address

Design updates this register on the rising edge of reset with predefined default values.

A connected PE writes this register to allocate a circular buffer while setting up a stream, whose size is
os_cb_end_adr - os_cb_st_adr +1 bytes.

Buffer size should be a power of 2. 
To allocate a buffer size of K bytes, this bitfield should be programmed with os_cb_st_adr + K -1.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_INT_ACK
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Interrupt Acknowledge

Increments CB write pointer by the interrupt size, if interrupt is enabled and wrptr_ovd_dat_xmt_gsk  is set.

Can be set by software. Rising edge on the corresponding interrupt ACK pin is equivalent to setting this bit. In both cases, this bit is auto cleared by hardware.

Output Stream Interrupt is cleared. If both buffer and interrupt size interrupt sources are set, buffer size interrupt source is cleared first in the hardware.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_WRPTR_OVD_DAT_XMT_GSK
Fields.Description: Output Stream Write Pointer Override Data Transmit Gasket
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Write Pointer Override at the Data transmit Gasket.

Used by source gasket for auto stream allocation.

0: Write pointer increments when data is written to the circular buffer. Ignores PE's addressing mode.

1: Write pointer is incremented by interrupt size on Return from Interrupt. Obeys PE's addressing mode.

    Indirect access Mode: Write pointer does not change.

    FIFO access Mode: Write pointer increments.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_C_BCAST_EN
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Cascaded Broadcast Enable

This broadcast is used to implement forking.

Fields.Name: OS_C_BCAST_FLAG
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Cascaded Broadcast Flag

0: Use the broadcast unit to implement forking.

1: Send every transaction individually to each destination.

Fields.Name: OS_STRM_PRIO
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Priority.

Prioritize stream traffic on AXI bus. This also applies to the arbitration inside the gasket.

00: Default priority.

1,2: Higher priority.

3: Highest priority.

Streams at the same priority level are arbitrated using round robin scheme.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_DST_GASKET_ID
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Destination Gasket ID.

This specifies ID of the gasket receiving the data transmitted by this gasket.

This field is ignored when cascaded broadcast enable is set.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Can be set by software. Rising edge on the corresponding interrupt ACK pin is equivalent to setting this bit. In both cases, this bit is auto cleared by hardware.

Output Stream Interrupt is cleared. If both buffer and interrupt size interrupt sources are set, buffer size interrupt source is cleared first in the hardware.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_WRPTR_OVD_DAT_XMT_GSK
Fields.Description: Output Stream Write Pointer Override Data Transmit Gasket
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Write Pointer Override at the Data transmit Gasket.

Used by source gasket for auto stream allocation.

0: Write pointer increments when data is written to the circular buffer. Ignores PE's addressing mode.

1: Write pointer is incremented by interrupt size on Return from Interrupt. Obeys PE's addressing mode.

    Indirect access Mode: Write pointer does not change.

    FIFO access Mode: Write pointer increments.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_C_BCAST_EN
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Cascaded Broadcast Enable

This broadcast is used to implement forking.

Fields.Name: OS_C_BCAST_FLAG
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Cascaded Broadcast Flag

0: Use the broadcast unit to implement forking.

1: Send every transaction individually to each destination.

Fields.Name: OS_STRM_PRIO
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Priority.

Prioritize stream traffic on AXI bus. This also applies to the arbitration inside the gasket.

00: Default priority.

1,2: Higher priority.

3: Highest priority.

Streams at the same priority level are arbitrated using round robin scheme.

Output Stream Write Pointer Override at the Data transmit Gasket.

Used by source gasket for auto stream allocation.

0: Write pointer increments when data is written to the circular buffer. Ignores PE's addressing mode.

1: Write pointer is incremented by interrupt size on Return from Interrupt. Obeys PE's addressing mode.

    Indirect access Mode: Write pointer does not change.

    FIFO access Mode: Write pointer increments.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_C_BCAST_EN
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Cascaded Broadcast Enable

This broadcast is used to implement forking.

Fields.Name: OS_C_BCAST_FLAG
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Cascaded Broadcast Flag

0: Use the broadcast unit to implement forking.

1: Send every transaction individually to each destination.

Fields.Name: OS_STRM_PRIO
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Priority.

Prioritize stream traffic on AXI bus. This also applies to the arbitration inside the gasket.

00: Default priority.

1,2: Higher priority.

3: Highest priority.

Streams at the same priority level are arbitrated using round robin scheme.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_DST_GASKET_ID
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Destination Gasket ID.

This specifies ID of the gasket receiving the data transmitted by this gasket.

This field is ignored when cascaded broadcast enable is set.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_INT_ACK
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Interrupt Acknowledge

Increments CB write pointer by the interrupt size, if interrupt is enabled and wrptr_ovd_dat_xmt_gsk  is set.

Can be set by software. Rising edge on the corresponding interrupt ACK pin is equivalent to setting this bit. In both cases, this bit is auto cleared by hardware.

Output Stream Interrupt is cleared. If both buffer and interrupt size interrupt sources are set, buffer size interrupt source is cleared first in the hardware.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_STRM_ID
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream ID

Stream ID is virtual and not related to gasket ids. They are generated and maintained by software.
For proper gasket operation, following should be followed.

 -- All ID’s used by output streams should be unique in a gasket

 -- All ID’s used by input streams should be unique in a gasket.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_SRC_INT_EN
Fields.Description: Output Stream Data Source Interrupt Enable
Fields.Documentation: Output Stream Data Source Interrupt Enable.

Fields.Name: OS_SRC_INT_TRG
Fields.Description: Output Stream Data Source Interrupt Triggers
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Data Source Interrupt Triggers

Bit0: Reserved.

Bit1: Reserved.

Bit2: Interrupt on interrupt size. Interrupt issued whenever data of “interrupt size” is transmitted. When buffer size interrupt is enabled, first interrupt for this type happens when a data of buffer plus interrupt size is transmitted.

Bit3: Reserved.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Data Source Interrupt Triggers

Bit0: Reserved.

Bit1: Reserved.

Bit2: Interrupt on interrupt size. Interrupt issued whenever data of “interrupt size” is transmitted. When buffer size interrupt is enabled, first interrupt for this type happens when a data of buffer plus interrupt size is transmitted.

Bit3: Reserved.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_STRM_ID
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream ID

Stream ID is virtual and not related to gasket ids. They are generated and maintained by software.
For proper gasket operation, following should be followed.

 -- All ID’s used by output streams should be unique in a gasket

 -- All ID’s used by input streams should be unique in a gasket.

Fields.Name: IS_CB_END_ADR

Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Circular Buffer End Address

Design updates this register on the rising edge of reset with predefined default values.

A connected PE writes this register to allocate a circular buffer while setting up a stream, whose size is
is_cb_end_adr - is_cb_st_adr.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_INT_ACK
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Interrupt Acknowledge

Decrements Interrupt Counter.
Increments CB read pointer by the interrupt size if interrupt is enabled and rdptr_ovd_dat_rcv_gsk is set.
This may effectively clear the interrupt if data available in the buffer falls below the thresholds set by buffer size and interrupt size.

Can be set by software. Rising edge on the corresponding interrupt ACK pin is equivalent to setting this bit. In both cases, this bit is auto cleared by hardware.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_RDPTR_OVD_DAT_RCV_GSK
Fields.Description: Input Stream Read Pointer Override Data Receive Gasket
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Read Pointer Override at the Data receive Gasket. (This gasket)

0: Read pointer is incremented by interrupt size on Return from Interrupt. Obeys PE's addressing mode.
    Indirect access Mode: Read pointer does not change.
    FIFO access Mode: Read pointer increments.

1: Read pointer increments when data is read from the circular buffer. Ignores PE's addressing mode.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_STRM_PRIO
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Priority.

Prioritize stream traffic on AXI bus. This also applies to the arbitration inside the gasket.

00: Default priority.

1,2: Higher priority.

3: Highest priority.

Streams at the same priority level are arbitrated using round robin scheme.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Description: Input Stream Read Pointer Override Data Receive Gasket
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Read Pointer Override at the Data receive Gasket. (This gasket)

0: Read pointer is incremented by interrupt size on Return from Interrupt. Obeys PE's addressing mode.
    Indirect access Mode: Read pointer does not change.
    FIFO access Mode: Read pointer increments.

1: Read pointer increments when data is read from the circular buffer. Ignores PE's addressing mode.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_STRM_PRIO
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Priority.

Prioritize stream traffic on AXI bus. This also applies to the arbitration inside the gasket.

00: Default priority.

1,2: Higher priority.

3: Highest priority.

Streams at the same priority level are arbitrated using round robin scheme.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_INT_ACK
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Interrupt Acknowledge

Decrements Interrupt Counter.
Increments CB read pointer by the interrupt size if interrupt is enabled and rdptr_ovd_dat_rcv_gsk is set.
This may effectively clear the interrupt if data available in the buffer falls below the thresholds set by buffer size and interrupt size.

Can be set by software. Rising edge on the corresponding interrupt ACK pin is equivalent to setting this bit. In both cases, this bit is auto cleared by hardware.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_STRM_ID
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream ID

Stream ID is virtual and not related to gasket ids. They are generated and maintained by software.
For proper gasket operation, following should be followed.

 -- All ID’s used by output streams should be unique in a gasket

 -- All ID’s used by input streams should be unique in a gasket.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_DST_INT_EN
Fields.Description: Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Enable
Fields.Documentation: Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Enable.

Fields.Name: IS_DST_INT_TRG
Fields.Description: Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Triggers
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Triggers

Bit0: Reserved.

Bit1: Interrupt on buffer size. First interrupt after stream is started happens when data of “buffer size” is received. Subsequent interrupts are issued whenever data of “interrupt size” is received. 

Bit2: Interrupt on interrupt size. Interrupt issued whenever data of “interrupt size” is received. 

Bit3: Reserved.

Only one bit should be set high for proper operation.

Bit3: Reserved.

Only one bit should be set high for proper operation.

Fields.Name: IS_STRM_ID
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream ID

Stream ID is virtual and not related to gasket ids. They are generated and maintained by software.
For proper gasket operation, following should be followed.

 -- All ID’s used by output streams should be unique in a gasket

 -- All ID’s used by input streams should be unique in a gasket.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_DST_INT_SIZE
Fields.Description: Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Size
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Size in bytes.

Interrupt is asserted after number of bytes specified by this register are received, if enabled. 

Circular Buffer Read pointer is incremented by interrupt size on Return from Interrupt. This behavior can be overridden by setting rdptr_ovd_dat_rcv_gsk bit; when set the read pointer increments when data is read from the circular buffer.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_BUFFER_SIZE
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Buffer Size in bytes

(E.g., 39 tap filter: 39 words * chunk size)

Interrupt is asserted after number of bytes specified by this register are received, if enabled. This happens only for the first buffer of this size in the stream. This bitfield can also be used for non-filter related streams to assert interrupts.

Register streams: Size of the data to write or read.

Fields.Name: OS_BCAST_MASK
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Broadcast Destination Mask

Design can update it after receiving the header.

1-bit per gasket. 
Set to send the packet to a destination gasket. E.g., 64’h0f0, sends packet to gasket with ids 4,5,6 and 7.

Fields.Name: OS_BCAST_MASK
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Broadcast Destination Mask

Design can update it after receiving the header.

1-bit per gasket. 
Set to send the packet to a destination gasket. E.g., 64’h0f0, sends packet to gasket with ids 4,5,6 and 7.

Registers.Name: ESS_DFG_OS_CB_ENDADR0
Registers.Description: Output Stream Circular Buffer End Address

Fields.Name: OS_CB_END_ADR
Fields.Description: Output Stream Circular Buffer End Address
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Circular Buffer End Address

Design updates this register on the rising edge of reset with predefined default values.

A connected PE writes this register to allocate a circular buffer while setting up a stream, whose size is
os_cb_end_adr - os_cb_st_adr +1 bytes.

Buffer size should be a power of 2. 
To allocate a buffer size of K bytes, this bitfield should be programmed with os_cb_st_adr + K -1.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_INT_ACK
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Interrupt Acknowledge

Increments CB write pointer by the interrupt size, if interrupt is enabled and wrptr_ovd_dat_xmt_gsk  is set.

Can be set by software. Rising edge on the corresponding interrupt ACK pin is equivalent to setting this bit. In both cases, this bit is auto cleared by hardware.

Output Stream Interrupt is cleared. If both buffer and interrupt size interrupt sources are set, buffer size interrupt source is cleared first in the hardware.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_WRPTR_OVD_DAT_XMT_GSK
Fields.Description: Output Stream Write Pointer Override Data Transmit Gasket
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Write Pointer Override at the Data transmit Gasket.

Used by source gasket for auto stream allocation.

0: Write pointer increments when data is written to the circular buffer. Ignores PE's addressing mode.

1: Write pointer is incremented by interrupt size on Return from Interrupt. Obeys PE's addressing mode.

    Indirect access Mode: Write pointer does not change.

    FIFO access Mode: Write pointer increments.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_C_BCAST_EN
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Cascaded Broadcast Enable

This broadcast is used to implement forking.

Fields.Name: OS_C_BCAST_FLAG
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Cascaded Broadcast Flag

0: Use the broadcast unit to implement forking.

1: Send every transaction individually to each destination.

Fields.Name: OS_STRM_PRIO
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Priority.

Prioritize stream traffic on AXI bus. This also applies to the arbitration inside the gasket.

00: Default priority.

1,2: Higher priority.

3: Highest priority.

Streams at the same priority level are arbitrated using round robin scheme.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_DST_GASKET_ID
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Destination Gasket ID.

This specifies ID of the gasket receiving the data transmitted by this gasket.

This field is ignored when cascaded broadcast enable is set.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Prioritize stream traffic on AXI bus. This also applies to the arbitration inside the gasket.

00: Default priority.

1,2: Higher priority.

3: Highest priority.

Streams at the same priority level are arbitrated using round robin scheme.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_DST_GASKET_ID
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Destination Gasket ID.

This specifies ID of the gasket receiving the data transmitted by this gasket.

This field is ignored when cascaded broadcast enable is set.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_INT_ACK
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Interrupt Acknowledge

Increments CB write pointer by the interrupt size, if interrupt is enabled and wrptr_ovd_dat_xmt_gsk  is set.

Can be set by software. Rising edge on the corresponding interrupt ACK pin is equivalent to setting this bit. In both cases, this bit is auto cleared by hardware.

Output Stream Interrupt is cleared. If both buffer and interrupt size interrupt sources are set, buffer size interrupt source is cleared first in the hardware.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_WRPTR_OVD_DAT_XMT_GSK
Fields.Description: Output Stream Write Pointer Override Data Transmit Gasket
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Write Pointer Override at the Data transmit Gasket.

Used by source gasket for auto stream allocation.

0: Write pointer increments when data is written to the circular buffer. Ignores PE's addressing mode.

1: Write pointer is incremented by interrupt size on Return from Interrupt. Obeys PE's addressing mode.

    Indirect access Mode: Write pointer does not change.

    FIFO access Mode: Write pointer increments.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_C_BCAST_EN
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Cascaded Broadcast Enable

This broadcast is used to implement forking.

Fields.Name: OS_C_BCAST_FLAG
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Cascaded Broadcast Flag

0: Use the broadcast unit to implement forking.

1: Send every transaction individually to each destination.

Fields.Name: OS_STRM_ID
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream ID

Stream ID is virtual and not related to gasket ids. They are generated and maintained by software.
For proper gasket operation, following should be followed.

 -- All ID’s used by output streams should be unique in a gasket

 -- All ID’s used by input streams should be unique in a gasket.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_SRC_INT_EN
Fields.Description: Output Stream Data Source Interrupt Enable
Fields.Documentation: Output Stream Data Source Interrupt Enable.

Fields.Name: OS_SRC_INT_TRG
Fields.Description: Output Stream Data Source Interrupt Triggers
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Data Source Interrupt Triggers

Bit0: Reserved.

Bit1: Reserved.

Bit2: Interrupt on interrupt size. Interrupt issued whenever data of “interrupt size” is transmitted. When buffer size interrupt is enabled, first interrupt for this type happens when a data of buffer plus interrupt size is transmitted.

Bit3: Reserved.

Fields.Name: GASKET_ID
Fields.Documentation: Gasket ID.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: TCM_WRSTALL_PRE_EN
Fields.Documentation: Enable TCM Write Stall. Stall the response phase of the write that makes the stream buffer full.

Fields.Name: TCM_WRSTALL_POST_EN
Fields.Documentation: Enable TCM Write Stall. Stall the response phase of the write that attempts a write to a full stream buffer.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_INT_TYPE
Fields.Documentation:
0: Level triggered interrupt
1: Edge triggered interrupt.

Fields.Name: OS_INT_TYPE
Fields.Documentation:
0: Level triggered interrupt
1: Edge triggered interrupt.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: STRM_PRIO_DISABLE
Fields.Documentation:
Disable Stream priority setting on all streams.
Use existing simple priority scheme. 

This is a temporary DEBUG bit, which will be removed in next RTL release.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Registers.Name: FSS_DFG_IS_CB_ENDADR0
Registers.Description: Input Stream Circular Buffer End Address

Fields.Name: IS_CB_END_ADR
Fields.Description: Input Stream Circular Buffer End Address
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Circular Buffer End Address

Design updates this register on the rising edge of reset with predefined default values.

A connected PE writes this register to allocate a circular buffer while setting up a stream, whose size is
is_cb_end_adr - is_cb_st_adr.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_CB_END_ADR
Fields.Description: Input Stream Circular Buffer End Address
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Circular Buffer End Address

Design updates this register on the rising edge of reset with predefined default values.

A connected PE writes this register to allocate a circular buffer while setting up a stream, whose size is
is_cb_end_adr - is_cb_st_adr.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_INT_ACK
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Interrupt Acknowledge

Decrements Interrupt Counter.
Increments CB read pointer by the interrupt size if interrupt is enabled and rdptr_ovd_dat_rcv_gsk is set.
This may effectively clear the interrupt if data available in the buffer falls below the thresholds set by buffer size and interrupt size.

Can be set by software. Rising edge on the corresponding interrupt ACK pin is equivalent to setting this bit. In both cases, this bit is auto cleared by hardware.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_RDPTR_OVD_DAT_RCV_GSK
Fields.Description: Input Stream Read Pointer Override Data Receive Gasket
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Read Pointer Override at the Data receive Gasket. (This gasket)

0: Read pointer is incremented by interrupt size on Return from Interrupt. Obeys PE's addressing mode.
    Indirect access Mode: Read pointer does not change.
    FIFO access Mode: Read pointer increments.

1: Read pointer increments when data is read from the circular buffer. Ignores PE's addressing mode.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_STRM_PRIO
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Priority.

Prioritize stream traffic on AXI bus. This also applies to the arbitration inside the gasket.

00: Default priority.

1,2: Higher priority.

3: Highest priority.

Streams at the same priority level are arbitrated using round robin scheme.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

0: Read pointer is incremented by interrupt size on Return from Interrupt. Obeys PE's addressing mode.
    Indirect access Mode: Read pointer does not change.
    FIFO access Mode: Read pointer increments.

1: Read pointer increments when data is read from the circular buffer. Ignores PE's addressing mode.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_STRM_PRIO
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Priority.

Prioritize stream traffic on AXI bus. This also applies to the arbitration inside the gasket.

00: Default priority.

1,2: Higher priority.

3: Highest priority.

Streams at the same priority level are arbitrated using round robin scheme.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_INT_ACK
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Interrupt Acknowledge

Decrements Interrupt Counter.
Increments CB read pointer by the interrupt size if interrupt is enabled and rdptr_ovd_dat_rcv_gsk is set.
This may effectively clear the interrupt if data available in the buffer falls below the thresholds set by buffer size and interrupt size.

Can be set by software. Rising edge on the corresponding interrupt ACK pin is equivalent to setting this bit. In both cases, this bit is auto cleared by hardware.

Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_STRM_ID
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream ID

Stream ID is virtual and not related to gasket ids. They are generated and maintained by software.
For proper gasket operation, following should be followed.

 -- All ID’s used by output streams should be unique in a gasket

 -- All ID’s used by input streams should be unique in a gasket.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_DST_INT_EN
Fields.Description: Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Enable
Fields.Documentation: Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Enable.

Fields.Name: IS_DST_INT_TRG
Fields.Description: Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Triggers
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Triggers

Bit0: Reserved.

Bit1: Interrupt on buffer size. First interrupt after stream is started happens when data of “buffer size” is received. Subsequent interrupts are issued whenever data of “interrupt size” is received. 

Bit2: Interrupt on interrupt size. Interrupt issued whenever data of “interrupt size” is received. 

Bit3: Reserved.

Only one bit should be set high for proper operation.

Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream ID

Stream ID is virtual and not related to gasket ids. They are generated and maintained by software.
For proper gasket operation, following should be followed.

 -- All ID’s used by output streams should be unique in a gasket

 -- All ID’s used by input streams should be unique in a gasket.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_DST_INT_EN
Fields.Description: Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Enable
Fields.Documentation: Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Enable.

Fields.Name: IS_DST_INT_TRG
Fields.Description: Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Triggers
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Triggers

Bit0: Reserved.

Bit1: Interrupt on buffer size. First interrupt after stream is started happens when data of “buffer size” is received. Subsequent interrupts are issued whenever data of “interrupt size” is received. 

Bit2: Interrupt on interrupt size. Interrupt issued whenever data of “interrupt size” is received. 

Bit3: Reserved.

Only one bit should be set high for proper operation.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_DST_INT_SIZE
Fields.Description: Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Size
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Size in bytes.

Interrupt is asserted after number of bytes specified by this register are received, if enabled. 

Circular Buffer Read pointer is incremented by interrupt size on Return from Interrupt. This behavior can be overridden by setting rdptr_ovd_dat_rcv_gsk bit; when set the read pointer increments when data is read from the circular buffer.

Fields.Description: Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Size
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Size in bytes.

Interrupt is asserted after number of bytes specified by this register are received, if enabled. 

Circular Buffer Read pointer is incremented by interrupt size on Return from Interrupt. This behavior can be overridden by setting rdptr_ovd_dat_rcv_gsk bit; when set the read pointer increments when data is read from the circular buffer.

Fields.Name: RESERVED

Fields.Name: IS_BUFFER_SIZE
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Buffer Size in bytes

(E.g., 39 tap filter: 39 words * chunk size)

Interrupt is asserted after number of bytes specified by this register are received, if enabled. This happens only for the first buffer of this size in the stream. This bitfield can also be used for non-filter related streams to assert interrupts.

Register streams: Size of the data to write or read.

Fields.Name: OS_BCAST_MASK
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Broadcast Destination Mask

Design can update it after receiving the header.

1-bit per gasket. 
Set to send the packet to a destination gasket. E.g., 64’h0f0, sends packet to gasket with ids 4,5,6 and 7.

Registers.Name: FSS_DFG_OS_CB_ENDADR0
Registers.Description: Output Stream Circular Buffer End Address

Fields.Name: OS_CB_END_ADR
Fields.Description: Output Stream Circular Buffer End Address
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Circular Buffer End Address

Design updates this register on the rising edge of reset with predefined default values.

A connected PE writes this register to allocate a circular buffer while setting up a stream, whose size is
os_cb_end_adr - os_cb_st_adr +1 bytes.

Buffer size should be a power of 2. 
To allocate a buffer size of K bytes, this bitfield should be programmed with os_cb_st_adr + K -1.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Description: Output Stream Circular Buffer End Address
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Circular Buffer End Address

Design updates this register on the rising edge of reset with predefined default values.

A connected PE writes this register to allocate a circular buffer while setting up a stream, whose size is
os_cb_end_adr - os_cb_st_adr +1 bytes.

Buffer size should be a power of 2. 
To allocate a buffer size of K bytes, this bitfield should be programmed with os_cb_st_adr + K -1.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_INT_ACK
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Interrupt Acknowledge

Increments CB write pointer by the interrupt size, if interrupt is enabled and wrptr_ovd_dat_xmt_gsk  is set.

Can be set by software. Rising edge on the corresponding interrupt ACK pin is equivalent to setting this bit. In both cases, this bit is auto cleared by hardware.

Output Stream Interrupt is cleared. If both buffer and interrupt size interrupt sources are set, buffer size interrupt source is cleared first in the hardware.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_WRPTR_OVD_DAT_XMT_GSK
Fields.Description: Output Stream Write Pointer Override Data Transmit Gasket
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Write Pointer Override at the Data transmit Gasket.

Used by source gasket for auto stream allocation.

0: Write pointer increments when data is written to the circular buffer. Ignores PE's addressing mode.

1: Write pointer is incremented by interrupt size on Return from Interrupt. Obeys PE's addressing mode.

    Indirect access Mode: Write pointer does not change.

    FIFO access Mode: Write pointer increments.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_C_BCAST_EN
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Cascaded Broadcast Enable

This broadcast is used to implement forking.

Fields.Name: OS_C_BCAST_FLAG
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Cascaded Broadcast Flag

0: Use the broadcast unit to implement forking.

1: Send every transaction individually to each destination.

Fields.Name: OS_STRM_PRIO
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Priority.

Prioritize stream traffic on AXI bus. This also applies to the arbitration inside the gasket.

00: Default priority.

1,2: Higher priority.

3: Highest priority.

Streams at the same priority level are arbitrated using round robin scheme.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_DST_GASKET_ID
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Destination Gasket ID.

This specifies ID of the gasket receiving the data transmitted by this gasket.

This field is ignored when cascaded broadcast enable is set.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Output Stream Priority.

Prioritize stream traffic on AXI bus. This also applies to the arbitration inside the gasket.

00: Default priority.

1,2: Higher priority.

3: Highest priority.

Streams at the same priority level are arbitrated using round robin scheme.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_DST_GASKET_ID
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Destination Gasket ID.

This specifies ID of the gasket receiving the data transmitted by this gasket.

This field is ignored when cascaded broadcast enable is set.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_INT_ACK
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Interrupt Acknowledge

Increments CB write pointer by the interrupt size, if interrupt is enabled and wrptr_ovd_dat_xmt_gsk  is set.

Can be set by software. Rising edge on the corresponding interrupt ACK pin is equivalent to setting this bit. In both cases, this bit is auto cleared by hardware.

Output Stream Interrupt is cleared. If both buffer and interrupt size interrupt sources are set, buffer size interrupt source is cleared first in the hardware.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_WRPTR_OVD_DAT_XMT_GSK
Fields.Description: Output Stream Write Pointer Override Data Transmit Gasket
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Write Pointer Override at the Data transmit Gasket.

Used by source gasket for auto stream allocation.

0: Write pointer increments when data is written to the circular buffer. Ignores PE's addressing mode.

1: Write pointer is incremented by interrupt size on Return from Interrupt. Obeys PE's addressing mode.

    Indirect access Mode: Write pointer does not change.

    FIFO access Mode: Write pointer increments.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_C_BCAST_EN
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Cascaded Broadcast Enable

This broadcast is used to implement forking.

Fields.Name: OS_C_BCAST_FLAG
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Cascaded Broadcast Flag

0: Use the broadcast unit to implement forking.

1: Send every transaction individually to each destination.

Streams at the same priority level are arbitrated using round robin scheme.

Fields.Name: OS_INT_ACK
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Interrupt Acknowledge

Increments CB write pointer by the interrupt size, if interrupt is enabled and wrptr_ovd_dat_xmt_gsk  is set.

Can be set by software. Rising edge on the corresponding interrupt ACK pin is equivalent to setting this bit. In both cases, this bit is auto cleared by hardware.

Output Stream Interrupt is cleared. If both buffer and interrupt size interrupt sources are set, buffer size interrupt source is cleared first in the hardware.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_WRPTR_OVD_DAT_XMT_GSK
Fields.Description: Output Stream Write Pointer Override Data Transmit Gasket
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Write Pointer Override at the Data transmit Gasket.

Used by source gasket for auto stream allocation.

0: Write pointer increments when data is written to the circular buffer. Ignores PE's addressing mode.

1: Write pointer is incremented by interrupt size on Return from Interrupt. Obeys PE's addressing mode.

    Indirect access Mode: Write pointer does not change.

    FIFO access Mode: Write pointer increments.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_C_BCAST_EN
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Cascaded Broadcast Enable

This broadcast is used to implement forking.

Fields.Name: OS_C_BCAST_FLAG
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Cascaded Broadcast Flag

0: Use the broadcast unit to implement forking.

1: Send every transaction individually to each destination.

Fields.Name: OS_STRM_PRIO
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Priority.

Prioritize stream traffic on AXI bus. This also applies to the arbitration inside the gasket.

00: Default priority.

1,2: Higher priority.

3: Highest priority.

Streams at the same priority level are arbitrated using round robin scheme.

Fields.Name: OS_STRM_ID
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream ID

Stream ID is virtual and not related to gasket ids. They are generated and maintained by software.
For proper gasket operation, following should be followed.

 -- All ID’s used by output streams should be unique in a gasket

 -- All ID’s used by input streams should be unique in a gasket.

Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_SRC_INT_EN
Fields.Description: Output Stream Data Source Interrupt Enable
Fields.Documentation: Output Stream Data Source Interrupt Enable.

Fields.Name: OS_SRC_INT_TRG
Fields.Description: Output Stream Data Source Interrupt Triggers
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Data Source Interrupt Triggers

Bit0: Reserved.

Bit1: Reserved.

Bit2: Interrupt on interrupt size. Interrupt issued whenever data of “interrupt size” is transmitted. When buffer size interrupt is enabled, first interrupt for this type happens when a data of buffer plus interrupt size is transmitted.

Bit3: Reserved.

is_cb_end_adr - is_cb_st_adr.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_INT_ACK
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Interrupt Acknowledge

Decrements Interrupt Counter.
Increments CB read pointer by the interrupt size if interrupt is enabled and rdptr_ovd_dat_rcv_gsk is set.
This may effectively clear the interrupt if data available in the buffer falls below the thresholds set by buffer size and interrupt size.

Can be set by software. Rising edge on the corresponding interrupt ACK pin is equivalent to setting this bit. In both cases, this bit is auto cleared by hardware.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_RDPTR_OVD_DAT_RCV_GSK
Fields.Description: Input Stream Read Pointer Override Data Receive Gasket
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Read Pointer Override at the Data receive Gasket. (This gasket)

0: Read pointer is incremented by interrupt size on Return from Interrupt. Obeys PE's addressing mode.
    Indirect access Mode: Read pointer does not change.
    FIFO access Mode: Read pointer increments.

1: Read pointer increments when data is read from the circular buffer. Ignores PE's addressing mode.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_STRM_PRIO
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Priority.

Prioritize stream traffic on AXI bus. This also applies to the arbitration inside the gasket.

00: Default priority.

1,2: Higher priority.

3: Highest priority.

Streams at the same priority level are arbitrated using round robin scheme.

Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_STRM_PRIO
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Priority.

Prioritize stream traffic on AXI bus. This also applies to the arbitration inside the gasket.

00: Default priority.

1,2: Higher priority.

3: Highest priority.

Streams at the same priority level are arbitrated using round robin scheme.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_INT_ACK
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Interrupt Acknowledge

Decrements Interrupt Counter.
Increments CB read pointer by the interrupt size if interrupt is enabled and rdptr_ovd_dat_rcv_gsk is set.
This may effectively clear the interrupt if data available in the buffer falls below the thresholds set by buffer size and interrupt size.

Can be set by software. Rising edge on the corresponding interrupt ACK pin is equivalent to setting this bit. In both cases, this bit is auto cleared by hardware.

    Indirect access Mode: Read pointer does not change.
    FIFO access Mode: Read pointer increments.

1: Read pointer increments when data is read from the circular buffer. Ignores PE's addressing mode.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_STRM_PRIO
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Priority.

Prioritize stream traffic on AXI bus. This also applies to the arbitration inside the gasket.

00: Default priority.

1,2: Higher priority.

3: Highest priority.

Streams at the same priority level are arbitrated using round robin scheme.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_INT_ACK
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Interrupt Acknowledge

Decrements Interrupt Counter.
Increments CB read pointer by the interrupt size if interrupt is enabled and rdptr_ovd_dat_rcv_gsk is set.
This may effectively clear the interrupt if data available in the buffer falls below the thresholds set by buffer size and interrupt size.

Can be set by software. Rising edge on the corresponding interrupt ACK pin is equivalent to setting this bit. In both cases, this bit is auto cleared by hardware.

Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_STRM_ID
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream ID

Stream ID is virtual and not related to gasket ids. They are generated and maintained by software.
For proper gasket operation, following should be followed.

 -- All ID’s used by output streams should be unique in a gasket

 -- All ID’s used by input streams should be unique in a gasket.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_DST_INT_EN
Fields.Description: Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Enable
Fields.Documentation: Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Enable.

Fields.Name: IS_DST_INT_TRG
Fields.Description: Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Triggers
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Triggers

Bit0: Reserved.

Bit1: Interrupt on buffer size. First interrupt after stream is started happens when data of “buffer size” is received. Subsequent interrupts are issued whenever data of “interrupt size” is received. 

Bit2: Interrupt on interrupt size. Interrupt issued whenever data of “interrupt size” is received. 

Bit3: Reserved.

Only one bit should be set high for proper operation.

Stream ID is virtual and not related to gasket ids. They are generated and maintained by software.
For proper gasket operation, following should be followed.

 -- All ID’s used by output streams should be unique in a gasket

 -- All ID’s used by input streams should be unique in a gasket.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_DST_INT_EN
Fields.Description: Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Enable
Fields.Documentation: Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Enable.

Fields.Name: IS_DST_INT_TRG
Fields.Description: Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Triggers
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Triggers

Bit0: Reserved.

Bit1: Interrupt on buffer size. First interrupt after stream is started happens when data of “buffer size” is received. Subsequent interrupts are issued whenever data of “interrupt size” is received. 

Bit2: Interrupt on interrupt size. Interrupt issued whenever data of “interrupt size” is received. 

Bit3: Reserved.

Only one bit should be set high for proper operation.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_DST_INT_SIZE
Fields.Description: Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Size
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Size in bytes.

Interrupt is asserted after number of bytes specified by this register are received, if enabled. 

Circular Buffer Read pointer is incremented by interrupt size on Return from Interrupt. This behavior can be overridden by setting rdptr_ovd_dat_rcv_gsk bit; when set the read pointer increments when data is read from the circular buffer.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_BUFFER_SIZE
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Buffer Size in bytes

(E.g., 39 tap filter: 39 words * chunk size)

Interrupt is asserted after number of bytes specified by this register are received, if enabled. This happens only for the first buffer of this size in the stream. This bitfield can also be used for non-filter related streams to assert interrupts.

Register streams: Size of the data to write or read.

Interrupt is asserted after number of bytes specified by this register are received, if enabled. This happens only for the first buffer of this size in the stream. This bitfield can also be used for non-filter related streams to assert interrupts.

Register streams: Size of the data to write or read.

Fields.Name: OS_BCAST_MASK
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Broadcast Destination Mask

Design can update it after receiving the header.

1-bit per gasket. 
Set to send the packet to a destination gasket. E.g., 64’h0f0, sends packet to gasket with ids 4,5,6 and 7.

Registers.Name: GSS_DFG_OS_CB_ENDADR0
Registers.Description: Output Stream Circular Buffer End Address

Fields.Name: OS_CB_END_ADR
Fields.Description: Output Stream Circular Buffer End Address
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Circular Buffer End Address

Design updates this register on the rising edge of reset with predefined default values.

A connected PE writes this register to allocate a circular buffer while setting up a stream, whose size is
os_cb_end_adr - os_cb_st_adr +1 bytes.

Buffer size should be a power of 2. 
To allocate a buffer size of K bytes, this bitfield should be programmed with os_cb_st_adr + K -1.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

A connected PE writes this register to allocate a circular buffer while setting up a stream, whose size is
os_cb_end_adr - os_cb_st_adr +1 bytes.

Buffer size should be a power of 2. 
To allocate a buffer size of K bytes, this bitfield should be programmed with os_cb_st_adr + K -1.

Registers.Description: Output Stream Circular Buffer Start Address

Fields.Name: OS_CB_ST_ADR
Fields.Description: Output Stream Circular Buffer Start Address
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Circular Buffer Start Address

Design updates this register on the rising edge of reset with predefined default values.

A connected PE writes this register to allocate a circular buffer while setting up a stream, whose size is
os_cb_end_adr - os_cb_st_adr + 1 bytes.

Buffer size should be a power of 2.
To allocate a buffer size of K bytes, this bitfield should be programmed with a value which is an integer multiple of K.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_INT_ACK
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Interrupt Acknowledge

Increments CB write pointer by the interrupt size, if interrupt is enabled and wrptr_ovd_dat_xmt_gsk  is set.

Can be set by software. Rising edge on the corresponding interrupt ACK pin is equivalent to setting this bit. In both cases, this bit is auto cleared by hardware.

Output Stream Interrupt is cleared. If both buffer and interrupt size interrupt sources are set, buffer size interrupt source is cleared first in the hardware.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_WRPTR_OVD_DAT_XMT_GSK
Fields.Description: Output Stream Write Pointer Override Data Transmit Gasket
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Write Pointer Override at the Data transmit Gasket.

Used by source gasket for auto stream allocation.

0: Write pointer increments when data is written to the circular buffer. Ignores PE's addressing mode.

1: Write pointer is incremented by interrupt size on Return from Interrupt. Obeys PE's addressing mode.

    Indirect access Mode: Write pointer does not change.

    FIFO access Mode: Write pointer increments.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_C_BCAST_EN
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Cascaded Broadcast Enable

This broadcast is used to implement forking.

Fields.Name: OS_C_BCAST_FLAG
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Cascaded Broadcast Flag

0: Use the broadcast unit to implement forking.

1: Send every transaction individually to each destination.

Fields.Name: OS_STRM_PRIO
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Priority.

Prioritize stream traffic on AXI bus. This also applies to the arbitration inside the gasket.

00: Default priority.

1,2: Higher priority.

3: Highest priority.

Streams at the same priority level are arbitrated using round robin scheme.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_DST_GASKET_ID
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Destination Gasket ID.

This specifies ID of the gasket receiving the data transmitted by this gasket.

This field is ignored when cascaded broadcast enable is set.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_STRM_ID
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream ID

Stream ID is virtual and not related to gasket ids. They are generated and maintained by software.
For proper gasket operation, following should be followed.

 -- All ID’s used by output streams should be unique in a gasket

 -- All ID’s used by input streams should be unique in a gasket.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_SRC_INT_EN
Fields.Description: Output Stream Data Source Interrupt Enable
Fields.Documentation: Output Stream Data Source Interrupt Enable.

Fields.Name: OS_SRC_INT_TRG
Fields.Description: Output Stream Data Source Interrupt Triggers
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Data Source Interrupt Triggers

Bit0: Reserved.

Bit1: Reserved.

Bit2: Interrupt on interrupt size. Interrupt issued whenever data of “interrupt size” is transmitted. When buffer size interrupt is enabled, first interrupt for this type happens when a data of buffer plus interrupt size is transmitted.

Bit3: Reserved.

Fields.Description: Output Stream Data Source Interrupt Triggers
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Data Source Interrupt Triggers

Bit0: Reserved.

Bit1: Reserved.

Bit2: Interrupt on interrupt size. Interrupt issued whenever data of “interrupt size” is transmitted. When buffer size interrupt is enabled, first interrupt for this type happens when a data of buffer plus interrupt size is transmitted.

Bit3: Reserved.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_STRM_ID
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream ID

Stream ID is virtual and not related to gasket ids. They are generated and maintained by software.
For proper gasket operation, following should be followed.

 -- All ID’s used by output streams should be unique in a gasket

 -- All ID’s used by input streams should be unique in a gasket.

is_cb_end_adr - is_cb_st_adr.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_INT_ACK
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Interrupt Acknowledge

Decrements Interrupt Counter.
Increments CB read pointer by the interrupt size if interrupt is enabled and rdptr_ovd_dat_rcv_gsk is set.
This may effectively clear the interrupt if data available in the buffer falls below the thresholds set by buffer size and interrupt size.

Can be set by software. Rising edge on the corresponding interrupt ACK pin is equivalent to setting this bit. In both cases, this bit is auto cleared by hardware.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_RDPTR_OVD_DAT_RCV_GSK
Fields.Description: Input Stream Read Pointer Override Data Receive Gasket
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Read Pointer Override at the Data receive Gasket. (This gasket)

0: Read pointer is incremented by interrupt size on Return from Interrupt. Obeys PE's addressing mode.
    Indirect access Mode: Read pointer does not change.
    FIFO access Mode: Read pointer increments.

1: Read pointer increments when data is read from the circular buffer. Ignores PE's addressing mode.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_STRM_PRIO
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Priority.

Prioritize stream traffic on AXI bus. This also applies to the arbitration inside the gasket.

00: Default priority.

1,2: Higher priority.

3: Highest priority.

Streams at the same priority level are arbitrated using round robin scheme.

Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_STRM_ID
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream ID

Stream ID is virtual and not related to gasket ids. They are generated and maintained by software.
For proper gasket operation, following should be followed.

 -- All ID’s used by output streams should be unique in a gasket

 -- All ID’s used by input streams should be unique in a gasket.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_DST_INT_EN
Fields.Description: Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Enable
Fields.Documentation: Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Enable.

Fields.Name: IS_DST_INT_TRG
Fields.Description: Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Triggers
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Triggers

Bit0: Reserved.

Bit1: Interrupt on buffer size. First interrupt after stream is started happens when data of “buffer size” is received. Subsequent interrupts are issued whenever data of “interrupt size” is received. 

Bit2: Interrupt on interrupt size. Interrupt issued whenever data of “interrupt size” is received. 

Bit3: Reserved.

Only one bit should be set high for proper operation.
