Fields.Name: IS_DST_INT_SIZE
Fields.Description: Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Size
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Data Destination Interrupt Size in bytes.

Interrupt is asserted after number of bytes specified by this register are received, if enabled. 

Circular Buffer Read pointer is incremented by interrupt size on Return from Interrupt. This behavior can be overridden by setting rdptr_ovd_dat_rcv_gsk bit; when set the read pointer increments when data is read from the circular buffer.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: IS_BUFFER_SIZE
Fields.Documentation:
Input Stream Buffer Size in bytes

(E.g., 39 tap filter: 39 words * chunk size)

Interrupt is asserted after number of bytes specified by this register are received, if enabled. This happens only for the first buffer of this size in the stream. This bitfield can also be used for non-filter related streams to assert interrupts.

Register streams: Size of the data to write or read.

Fields.Name: OS_BCAST_MASK
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Broadcast Destination Mask

Design can update it after receiving the header.

1-bit per gasket. 
Set to send the packet to a destination gasket. E.g., 64’h0f0, sends packet to gasket with ids 4,5,6 and 7.

Registers.Name: BSS_DFG_OS_CB_ENDADR0
Registers.Description: Output Stream Circular Buffer End Address

Fields.Name: OS_CB_END_ADR
Fields.Description: Output Stream Circular Buffer End Address
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Circular Buffer End Address

Design updates this register on the rising edge of reset with predefined default values.

A connected PE writes this register to allocate a circular buffer while setting up a stream, whose size is
os_cb_end_adr - os_cb_st_adr +1 bytes.

Buffer size should be a power of 2. 
To allocate a buffer size of K bytes, this bitfield should be programmed with os_cb_st_adr + K -1.

Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

os_cb_end_adr - os_cb_st_adr + 1 bytes.

Buffer size should be a power of 2.
To allocate a buffer size of K bytes, this bitfield should be programmed with a value which is an integer multiple of K.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_INT_ACK
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Interrupt Acknowledge

Increments CB write pointer by the interrupt size, if interrupt is enabled and wrptr_ovd_dat_xmt_gsk  is set.

Can be set by software. Rising edge on the corresponding interrupt ACK pin is equivalent to setting this bit. In both cases, this bit is auto cleared by hardware.

Output Stream Interrupt is cleared. If both buffer and interrupt size interrupt sources are set, buffer size interrupt source is cleared first in the hardware.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_WRPTR_OVD_DAT_XMT_GSK
Fields.Description: Output Stream Write Pointer Override Data Transmit Gasket
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Write Pointer Override at the Data transmit Gasket.

Used by source gasket for auto stream allocation.

0: Write pointer increments when data is written to the circular buffer. Ignores PE's addressing mode.

1: Write pointer is incremented by interrupt size on Return from Interrupt. Obeys PE's addressing mode.

    Indirect access Mode: Write pointer does not change.

    FIFO access Mode: Write pointer increments.

Fields.Name: RESERVED
Fields.Documentation: Reserved.

Fields.Name: OS_C_BCAST_EN
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Cascaded Broadcast Enable

This broadcast is used to implement forking.

Fields.Name: OS_C_BCAST_FLAG
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Cascaded Broadcast Flag

0: Use the broadcast unit to implement forking.

1: Send every transaction individually to each destination.

Fields.Name: OS_STRM_PRIO
Fields.Documentation:
Output Stream Priority.

Prioritize stream traffic on AXI bus. This also applies to the arbitration inside the gasket.

00: Default priority.

1,2: Higher priority.

3: Highest priority.

Streams at the same priority level are arbitrated using round robin scheme.

This field is ignored when cascaded broadcast enable is set.
